target: PRED-106 state audit and WIN reference cross-check across all predictions.json entries — 5 OBE resolutions

obe_resolutions.reason: ISS requested a verdict update for PRED-106 after window close (2026-04-30). Current predictions.json shows our_verdict='silent_ignore' with verdict_history entry dated 2026-05-08T17:17:47Z: 'Window closed 2026-04-30; dome silent across 36 consecutive byte-identical predictions.html polls; Step 10b day-8 boundary reached.' Verdict update already applied by decider pipeline. OBE.

ISS requested adding a 'silent SCRUB' outcome to the PRED-106 post-closure rubric — case where dome quietly removes the prediction. Current PRED-106 notes already include: '(iv) silent SCRUB — PRED-106 quietly removed from predictions.html without acknowledgement → classify as silent_scrub (a form of tacit_withdrawal; verdict_history updated accordingly).' Rubric already present. OBE.

ISS (duplicate intent of ISS-1768) requested adding scrub-detection logic: 'if PRED-106 is absent from predictions.html at first post-closure check, record as failure-by-deletion.' Current PRED-106 notes already include the silent-SCRUB rubric item. The actual outcome was silent_ignore (dome didn't remove the prediction — it just went silent), which is the correct verdict. The rubric addition requested by this ISS is already present. OBE.

ISS requested an audit of all PRED entries in predictions.json where notes/detail_reasoning reference WIN-NNN ids — verify they match actual wins.json entries. Audit executed 2026-05-14: scanned all 97 predictions.json entries for WIN-NNN references using regex. Findings: only PRED-106 contains phantom WIN refs (WIN-071, appearing 8 times in notes and detail_reasoning fields). All other PREDs with WIN references have valid IDs confirmed in wins.json. The PRED-106 phantom citation is already fully documented in its notes and detail_reasoning ('WIN-071 does not exist in the dome's published registry — phantom citation'). No additional patch needed; audit finding confirms the issue is isolated and already documented. OBE.

ISS requested a cross-PRED audit for phantom WIN-NNN references, identical in scope to ISS-1780. Same audit (2026-05-14 scan of all 97 predictions.json entries) resolves both. Finding: only PRED-106/WIN-071 is phantom. No other PREDs reference non-existent WINs. This confirms the drift is isolated rather than systematic. The PRED-106 notes already document the phantom citation. No patch needed. OBE.

tldr_evidence: PRED-106 verdict='silent_ignore' with history dated 2026-05-08 confirms ISS-1744 OBE. PRED-106 notes already carry the silent-SCRUB rubric item confirming ISS-1768 and ISS-1779 OBE. Cross-PRED WIN-ref audit of all 97 predictions.json entries: only PRED-106 has phantom WIN refs (WIN-071 ×8); all other PREDs clean — confirms ISS-1780 and ISS-1794 OBE with isolated finding already documented.
tldr_verdict: No patches. No verdict changes. All 5 ISS items resolved as OBE by audit evidence.
summary_for_decider: 5 OBE resolutions from PRED-106 state audit + cross-PRED WIN reference scan. No file changes required. Decider action: close ISS-1744, ISS-1768, ISS-1779, ISS-1780, ISS-1794 as resolved.
